title: Net-New Story Gap Review — 2026-05-15 (HRREC-82977)

HRREC-91989
PH: Recruiter can use the RTE & see the UI of buttons

31% — limited tension in this heuristic pass; lenses largely agree
  • Jira — Review BDD Scenarios vs compose/send/notification siblings for overlapping REST contract.
  • Salomon — (Replay placeholder—not live Salomon excerpts.) Run Salomon on this ticket’s exact wording in a live review; this replay only mirrors summary/gap keywords—not real hits.
  • Deployment Agent — (Replay placeholder—not live Deployment Agent answers.) In a live review, ask DA which admin toggles mirror common customer misconfiguration for this area.
  • Jira — Add one negative path tied to this summary.
  • Salomon — (Replay placeholder—not live Salomon excerpts.) In a live run, search Salomon on this key’s words—not a generic inbox checklist.

XO MCP — (Replay placeholder—condensed from stub XO text.) Same catalog snapshot as the row above (one epic-wide search in this replay). For HRREC-91989, relate the catalog hits above to the REST/UI slice in Summary—treat hits as direction, not shipped proof. Risk — Same catalog-level risk as rows above—confirm wiring for this key.
  • Finding: No strong cross-lens conflict showed up in this heuristic replay.
  • Recommended next step: Re-read Jira for “Rendering the Rich Text Editor and Toolbar” on HRREC-91989; if nothing new surfaces, keep scenarios as written.

Beyond Jira — Rendering the Rich Text Editor and Toolbar
Given A recruiter with Modify/Send on conversational email where this slice applies on a tenant staged for this epic; Jira’s first *Given* already includes: the email composition view is open in the slid...
When I open compose in the 2-way email task and edit To / From / Subject / body controls this story’s UI slice exposes
Then Observable UI, notification, or persisted thread state matches the acceptance criteria for this slice in recruiter-verifiable terms (harden wording under 430 / 319 before sign-off)

Replay note: Jira scenarios look ample and heuristic tension is low—no extra compact BDD blocks beyond the first scenario extension.

HRREC-91991
PH: Recruiter does not see any tabs for non-agency candidate

  • Jira — agency matrix — align with security domains and tab visibility siblings.
  • Salomon — (Replay placeholder—not live Salomon excerpts.) If precedent exists for agency submissions, plan tests that cover agency vs direct paths for this ticket.
  • Deployment Agent — (Replay placeholder—not live Deployment Agent answers.) In a live review, ask DA how to stage agency vs direct candidates for this slice.
  • Jira — Add one negative path tied to this summary.
  • Salomon — (Replay placeholder—not live Salomon excerpts.) In a live run, ask Salomon about agency duplicate threads only if this slice owns that risk.

Beyond Jira — Tabs are dynamically hidden for non-agency candidates

Agency vs non-agency predicates
Given the candidate has agency-linked and direct applications (or agency association toggles) as exercised in QA staging
When I open the email task and any tabs or compose entry points this story owns
Then chrome and permissions follow the portfolio rules for each case without thread bleed or misleading empty tabs

HRREC-91992
PH: Recruiter attaches non-supported files

  • Jira — attachments — align client vs server size/type rejection.
  • Salomon — (Replay placeholder—not live Salomon excerpts.) If internal threads mention attachment limits, align on-screen hints with what the system rejects for this slice.
  • Deployment Agent — (Replay placeholder—not live Deployment Agent answers.) In a live review, ask DA which admin toggles mirror common customer misconfiguration for this area.
  • Jira — Test attachment boundary errors this story owns.
  • Salomon — (Replay placeholder—not live Salomon excerpts.) In a live run, search Salomon on this key’s words—not a generic inbox checklist.

Beyond Jira — Client-side block for disallowed file extensions
Given A recruiter with Modify/Send on conversational email where this slice applies on a tenant staged for this epic; Jira’s first *Given* already includes: I attempt to select an attachment from my loca...
When I add, remove, or send with attachments at the boundary of the stated size/type rules for this ticket
Then Client and server outcomes match recruiter-visible error copy and thread contents at the stated attachment limits

Attachment limits client and server
Given I stage allowed and disallowed files and sizes around the published limit
When I attempt send
Then client validation, server rejection, and recruiter-visible error strings stay aligned (no orphan partial sends)
